opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Parent) These little bumps have been on my child's groin for months, but today the whole area has turned bright red, swollen and it's too painful for them to walk normally. I'm really worried it's turning into a bad spreading infection.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The bumps have been there and harmless for months, but over the last day or two the skin around some of them suddenly went red, puffy and sore, and my child is limping to keep their clothes off it.

symptom_narrative.onset: the bumps themselves started about 4 months ago; the redness, swelling and soreness came on suddenly over the last 48 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: bumps ~4 months; the red inflamed flare ~2 days
symptom_narrative.character: a cluster of small firm dome-shaped bumps that were painless for months; now several of them are surrounded by a patch of bright red, slightly puffy, tender skin, with a dry flaky rash ringing the area
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the child is sore and mildly distressed when the area is pressed, but is comfortable sitting still
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the right groin crease and the top of the inner thigh
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the redness appeared over the last day or so and has stayed around the bumps
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: keeping clothing off the area and sitting still
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: clothing rubbing on it when walking; scratching
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a slight limp to avoid the clothing rubbing; otherwise eating, drinking and playing normally
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no previous episodes like this; the bumps have simply been present and painless until now
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: walking with a slight limp to avoid friction; otherwise independent and active

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a cluster of small skin bumps in the groin for about 4 months, not previously seen by a doctor

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young child, lives at home; parent present and very anxious about a spreading infection

objective_data_offered.value: no fever - felt normal, home thermometer around 37 degrees
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single home thermometer reading reported by the parent

objective_data_offered.value: a cluster of roughly 10-15 small firm bumps in the right groin crease and inner thigh; several are now surrounded by a coin-sized patch of bright red, slightly swollen skin; a dry flaky rash rings the whole cluster

child is alert and comfortable sitting, walking with a slight limp, mildly upset only when the sore area is touched; no pus or weeping seen; the redness is staying around the bumps and not racing down the leg
